error.signature: toomanyrequests: You have reached your pull rate limit. You may increase the limit by authenticating.

verdict.summary: Docker Hub limits anonymous pulls to 100/6hr per IP, authenticated free to 200/6hr. CI/CD systems sharing an IP (NAT) quickly exhaust the limit. Kubernetes clusters pulling the same image from multiple nodes multiply the pull count.

dead_ends.action: Authenticate with Docker Hub to get unlimited pulls
dead_ends.why_fails: Authenticated free accounts get 200/6hr, not unlimited. Only paid Docker Pro/Team/Business get higher limits.

dead_ends.action: Pull images less frequently to stay under the limit
dead_ends.why_fails: In Kubernetes, each node independently pulls images. 10 nodes pulling on deploy = 10 pulls even if the image is the same.

workarounds.action: Use a registry mirror/proxy (Harbor, JFrog, AWS ECR pull-through cache)
workarounds.how: ECR pull-through cache: aws ecr create-pull-through-cache-rule --ecr-repository-prefix docker-hub --upstream-registry-url docker.io

workarounds.action: Copy frequently-used images to your private registry
workarounds.how: docker pull nginx:latest && docker tag nginx:latest myregistry.com/nginx:latest && docker push myregistry.com/nginx:latest

workarounds.action: Use imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent in Kubernetes to reduce pulls
workarounds.how: Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent and use specific tags (not :latest) to leverage node-level image cache
